title: Automated Testing with GitHub Actions
text: Without renv, routine package update silently change coefficient table. Without snapshots, tests catch numerical drift. Without multi-OS CI, platform-specific floating-point differences go unnoticed.

title: Automated Testing with GitHub Actions
text: minimal project uses workflow looks like: resultcheck.yml file root can empty — presence enough find_root() locate project:
code: myproject/ ├── .Rprofile                   # auto-activates renv ├── renv.lock                   # locked package versions (committed) ├── renv/                       # renv internals (mostly gitignored) ├── resultcheck.yml             # marks the project root ├── data/ │   └── panel_data.rds ├── code/ │   └── analysis.R              # your analysis script ├── _resultcheck_snapshots/     # committed snapshot files │   └── analysis/ │       └── main_model.md └── tests/     └── testthat/         └── test-analysis.R # resultcheck.yml

title: Automated Testing with GitHub Actions
text: Inside R, project open: Install packages project needs, snapshot environment: Commit .Rprofile renv.lock. renv/ folder partially ignored according renv’s .gitignore (created automatically renv::init()).
code: install.packages("renv") renv::init() renv::install(c("resultcheck", "testthat")) # ... install any other packages your analysis uses ... renv::snapshot()

what: Step 3 — Add snapshots to your analysis script
title: Automated Testing with GitHub Actions
text: Call resultcheck::snapshot() every object whose value matters reproducibility. first time run script interactively snapshot saved; subsequent runs compared saved version. Run script interactively generate .md snapshot files, review , commit version control.
code: # code/analysis.R data  <- readRDS("data/panel_data.rds") model <- lm(y ~ x1 + x2, data = data)  resultcheck::snapshot(model,  "main_model") resultcheck::snapshot(data,   "panel_data")  # ... continue writing outputs ...

title: Automated Testing with GitHub Actions
text: Run locally confirm everything passes pushing:
code: # tests/testthat/test-analysis.R library(testthat) library(resultcheck)  test_that("analysis produces stable results", {   sandbox <- setup_sandbox("data")   on.exit(cleanup_sandbox(sandbox), add = TRUE)    # snapshot() inside run_in_sandbox() errors on any mismatch   run_in_sandbox("code/analysis.R", sandbox) }) testthat::test_dir("tests/testthat")

title: Automated Testing with GitHub Actions
text: Create .github/workflows/run-tests.yml. key ingredients : Matrix strategy — runs Windows, macOS, Ubuntu platform-specific numerical differences caught early. OS-specific system libraries — packages ragg, xml2, curl need native libraries Linux macOS required Windows. renv cache — r-lib/actions/setup-renv@v2 restores renv cache runs, avoiding re-installing hundreds packages every push.
code: name: R Tests  on:   push:     branches: [ main ]   pull_request:     branches: [ main ]   workflow_dispatch:  jobs:   test:     runs-on: ${{ matrix.config.os }}     name: ${{ matrix.config.os }}      strategy:       fail-fast: false       matrix:         config:           - {os: windows-latest}           - {os: ubuntu-latest}           - {os: macos-latest}      env:       GITHUB_PAT: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}       R_KEEP_PKG_SOURCE: yes      steps:       - name: Checkout repository         uses: actions/checkout@v4        - name: Setup R         uses: r-lib/actions/setup-r@v2         with:           use-public-rspm: true        # ── Linux system libraries ──────────────────────────────────────────       - name: Install system dependencies (Linux)         if: runner.os == 'Linux'         run: |           sudo apt-get update           sudo apt-get install -y \             libcurl4-openssl-dev \             libssl-dev \             libxml2-dev \             libfontconfig1-dev \             libharfbuzz-dev \             libfribidi-dev \             libfreetype6-dev \             libpng-dev \             libtiff5-dev \             libjpeg-dev        # ── macOS system libraries ───────────────────────────────────────────       - name: Install system dependencies (macOS)         if: runner.os == 'macOS'         run: |           set -euxo pipefail           brew update           brew install pkg-config libpng cairo freetype harfbuzz fribidi            BREW_PREFIX="$(brew --prefix)"           echo "SDKROOT=$(xcrun --sdk macosx --show-sdk-path)" >> $GITHUB_ENV           echo "PATH=${BREW_PREFIX}/bin:${PATH}"               >> $GITHUB_ENV           echo "PKG_CONFIG_PATH=${BREW_PREFIX}/lib/pkgconfig:$(brew --prefix libpng)/lib/pkgconfig" >> $GITHUB_ENV            mkdir -p ~/.R           PNG_CFLAGS="$(pkg-config --cflags libpng)"           PNG_LIBS="$(pkg-config --libs   libpng)"           {             echo "CPPFLAGS += -I${BREW_PREFIX}/include"             echo "LDFLAGS  += -L${BREW_PREFIX}/lib -Wl,-rpath,${BREW_PREFIX}/lib"             echo "PKG_CPPFLAGS += ${PNG_CFLAGS}"             echo "PKG_LIBS     += ${PNG_LIBS}"           } >> ~/.R/Makevars        # ── Restore renv cache (fast!) ───────────────────────────────────────       - name: Restore renv packages         uses: r-lib/actions/setup-renv@v2         with:           cache-version: 2        # ── Run tests ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────       - name: Run tests         run: Rscript -e "testthat::test_dir('tests/testthat')"        - name: Upload test artefacts on failure         if: failure()         uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4         with:           name: test-results-${{ matrix.config.os }}           path: tests/testthat/*.Rout*

title: Automated Testing with GitHub Actions
text: r-lib/actions/setup-renv@v2 reads renv.lock file, computes cache key hash, restores previously saved cache installed packages calling renv::restore(). lock file changed, packages served cache renv::restore() completes seconds rather minutes. Increment cache-version (e.g. 2 3) whenever want force full re-install — example OS upgrade debugging strange linking error.

title: Automated Testing with GitHub Actions
text: CI never updates snapshots; enforces . accept legitimate result change, always re-run script interactively, confirm diff, commit updated .md files.
code: Developer                         CI runner ─────────                         ───────── 1. Edit analysis.R 2. Run interactively → snapshots    generated / updated 3. Review diffs, accept changes 4. git add _resultcheck_snapshots/    git commit && git push                                   5. Workflow triggered                                   6. renv::restore() (from cache)                                   7. testthat::test_dir()                                      └─ run_in_sandbox("code/analysis.R")                                         └─ snapshot() in *testing mode*                                            ✓ matches committed file → pass                                            ✗ differs             → FAIL

title: Automated Testing with GitHub Actions
text: computation yields slightly different floating-point values different operating systems, use mechanisms described vignette("snapshot-tolerance"): [ignored] markers — replace volatile line snapshot file literal text [ignored]. line position skipped every platform. snapshot.precision — add precision key resultcheck.yml round floating-point numbers comparison: Either option lets CI pass platforms without losing safety net lines matter.

title: Automated Testing with GitHub Actions
text: Commit renv.lock renv/library/. library rebuilt lock file runner; lock file needs version control. Keep snapshots human-readable. .md files produced snapshot() plain text diff well pull requests — reviewers can see glance whether coefficient changed. Pin R version workflow matrix (e.g. r-version: '4.4.2') minor R releases ever changed numerical results. fail-fast: false lets three OS jobs run completion even one fails, giving full picture discrepancy occurs. workflow_dispatch allows trigger workflow manually GitHub Actions UI — useful debugging without push commit.

what: The problem: floating-point noise across platforms
title: Handling Platform Differences in Snapshots
text: Numerical computations R can produce results differ high decimal places depending platform, compiler, version libraries like BLAS/LAPACK. example, centering attribute scale() might 2.41e-17 one machine 1.63e-17 another — effectively zero, naïve snapshot comparison treats different. resultcheck provides two complementary mechanisms handle : [ignored] line markers — mark specific lines snapshot file known discrepancies always skipped. snapshot.precision resultcheck.yml — round floating-point numbers fixed number decimal places comparing storing snapshots.

title: Handling Platform Differences in Snapshots
text: Open .md snapshot file _resultcheck_snapshots/ replace entire content volatile line literal text [ignored]. every subsequent run, line position masked comparison takes place, differences never trigger failure. update snapshot interactively, [ignored] markers automatically preserved new file.

title: Handling Platform Differences in Snapshots
text: Suppose PCA.R script produces snapshot includes lines like: values fluctuate slightly platforms. Open snapshot file _resultcheck_snapshots/PCA/panel_data_pca.md change specific lines : Save file. snapshot now looks like (abbreviated): now , run_in_sandbox("code/PCA.R", sandbox) raise error regardless two lines contain new run, every line continues verified.
code: ..- attr(*, "scaled:center")= num 2.41e-17   ..- attr(*, "scaled:center")= num 2.98e-17 [ignored] [ignored] # Snapshot: data.frame  ## Structure 'data.frame':  1071 obs. of  30 variables:  ...  $ us : num [1:1071, 1] NA -0.423 -0.697 ... [ignored]   ..- attr(*, "scaled:scale")= num 1.22  $ eu : num [1:1071, 1] NA -0.783 -0.711 ... [ignored]   ..- attr(*, "scaled:scale")= num 1.37

title: Handling Platform Differences in Snapshots
text: entire line must [ignored] (leading/trailing whitespace stripped check, indentation matter). Lines matched position within snapshot file, adding removing lines shifts positions. overall structure output changes materially, update snapshot interactively first, re-apply [ignored] markers new file. [ignored] markers preserved automatically update snapshot: resultcheck copies [ignored] line stored snapshot freshly generated one writing disk.

title: Handling Platform Differences in Snapshots
text: Add snapshot.precision key resultcheck.yml file root project. storing comparing snapshot, every floating-point number text representation rounded many decimal places. Integer-like tokens (e.g. index ranges [1:1071]) left unchanged. precision: 10, 2.41e-17 1.63e-17 round 0, snapshots compare equal.

title: Handling Platform Differences in Snapshots
text: Add precision resultcheck.yml: Delete outdated snapshot (run interactively accept update prompted) stored file regenerated rounded values. Re-run script via run_in_sandbox(). comparison now succeed whenever effective difference smaller 0.5 × 10^{-precision}.

title: Handling Platform Differences in Snapshots
text: higher value sensitive (closer exact comparison); lower value forgiving.

title: Handling Platform Differences in Snapshots
text: project already snapshot files stored precision configured, resultcheck also rounds stored snapshot numbers comparison. means setting precision immediately suppress floating-point noise existing snapshots without needing regenerate .

title: Handling Platform Differences in Snapshots
text: two features independent compose naturally: Use [ignored] line’s value entirely unreliable across runs (e.g. random seed, memory address, platform-specific constant carries scientific meaning). Use precision values meaningful may differ last decimal places due floating-point arithmetic. PCA example problem statement, either approach resolves failure. Using precision: 10 lower-maintenance option requires manual edits snapshot files; [ignored] offers surgical control handful lines volatile rest must exact.

what: Result Stability Checks for Empirical R Projects
title: Result Stability Checks for Empirical R Projects
text: Result Stability Checks Empirical R Projects

title: Result Stability Checks for Empirical R Projects
text: resultcheck provides lightweight helpers checking whether empirical results remain unchanged across code revisions, platform differences, package updates. Call snapshot() key outputs (models, tables, derived datasets) analysis scripts detect unintended result drift automatically CI local testing.

title: Result Stability Checks for Empirical R Projects
Result Stability Checks for Empirical R Projects
Result Stability Checks for Empirical R Projects
text: package supports two-phase workflow: Interactive development — run analysis script call snapshot() objects care . first run snapshot saved human-readable .md file. subsequent interactive runs, differences shown prompted update. Automated testing — wrap script setup_sandbox() / run_in_sandbox() / cleanup_sandbox(). Inside run_in_sandbox(), snapshot() switches testing mode: errors immediately snapshot missing changed, making test fail.

title: Result Stability Checks for Empirical R Projects
text: Consider project layout:
code: myproject/ ├── resultcheck.yml       # marks the project root ├── data/ │   └── income.csv        # input data ├── analysis.R            # analysis script └── tests/     └── testthat/         └── test-analysis.R

what: analysis.R — snapshot key results and write output
title: Result Stability Checks for Empirical R Projects
code: data <- read.csv("data/income.csv") model <- lm(income ~ age + education, data = data)  # Snapshot detects unexpected result changes across code revisions. # Interactive: warns and prompts to update when differences are found. # Inside run_in_sandbox(): errors if snapshot is missing or doesn't match. resultcheck::snapshot(model, "income_model")  # Write model summary to an output file dir.create("output", showWarnings = FALSE) write.csv(   as.data.frame(coef(summary(model))),   "output/model_summary.csv" )

title: Result Stability Checks for Empirical R Projects
text: analysis.R changes way alters model, run_in_sandbox() errors immediately. accept change, re-run script interactively, review diff, confirm update.
code: library(testthat) library(resultcheck)  test_that("analysis produces stable results", {    # Run the script in an isolated sandbox.   # Only input data is copied — snapshot files live at the project root and   # are located automatically by find_root(); you do not need to list them.   # You may pass an entire directory instead of individual file paths.   sandbox <- setup_sandbox("data")   on.exit(cleanup_sandbox(sandbox), add = TRUE)    # Errors immediately if any snapshot inside analysis.R doesn't match.   run_in_sandbox("analysis.R", sandbox)    # Verify output files were written.   expect_true(     file.exists(file.path(sandbox$path, "output", "model_summary.csv"))   ) })

what: snapshot(value, name, script_name = NULL, method = c("both", "print", "str"))
title: Result Stability Checks for Empirical R Projects
text: Creates verifies snapshot R object. First interactive run: saves object human-readable .md file _resultcheck_snapshots/<script>/ project root. Subsequent interactive runs: shows diff prompts update. Inside run_in_sandbox(): errors snapshot missing doesn’t match. method argument controls object serialized: Use "print" "str" one serialization method produces volatile output excluded snapshot (e.g. objects embed session-specific file paths random IDs str() representation). Snapshots plain text intended committed version control.

title: Result Stability Checks for Empirical R Projects
text: Creates temporary directory copies listed files /directories , preserving path structure relative project root. Directories copied recursively. Snapshot files need listed.

title: Result Stability Checks for Empirical R Projects
text: Runs R script inside sandbox. working directory set sandbox, find_root() snapshot() automatically resolve back original project root snapshots found correctly.

title: Result Stability Checks for Empirical R Projects
text: Removes sandbox directory. Omit argument clean recently created sandbox.

what: Round Floating-Point Numbers in Snapshot Text — round_snapshot_numbers
title: Round Floating-Point Numbers in Snapshot Text — round_snapshot_numbers
text: Replaces every floating-point literal (decimal scientific notation) found character vector snapshot lines value rounded digits decimal places.  Integer literals contain decimal point exponent left untouched, index ranges [1:1071] never modified.

title: Round Floating-Point Numbers in Snapshot Text — round_snapshot_numbers
Round Floating-Point Numbers in Snapshot Text — round_snapshot_numbers
text: text Character vector snapshot text lines. digits Integer; number decimal places keep (passed round()).  Must finite integer.

title: Round Floating-Point Numbers in Snapshot Text — round_snapshot_numbers
text: Character vector floating-point numbers rounded.

title: Round Floating-Point Numbers in Snapshot Text — round_snapshot_numbers
text: digits passed directly round: 0 rounds nearest integer, negative values round left decimal point.

what: Run Code in a Sandbox Environment — run_in_sandbox
title: Run Code in a Sandbox Environment — run_in_sandbox
text: Executes R script within sandbox directory, suppressing messages, warnings, graphical output. useful testing empirical analysis scripts without polluting console creating unwanted plots.

title: Run Code in a Sandbox Environment — run_in_sandbox
code: run_in_sandbox(   script_path,   sandbox = NULL,   suppress_messages = TRUE,   suppress_warnings = TRUE,   capture_output = TRUE )

title: Run Code in a Sandbox Environment — run_in_sandbox
text: script_path Path R script execute. sandbox Optional. sandbox object created setup_sandbox(). NULL (default), uses recently created sandbox. suppress_messages Logical. Whether suppress messages (default: TRUE). suppress_warnings Logical. Whether suppress warnings (default: TRUE). capture_output Logical. Whether capture output (default: TRUE).

title: Run Code in a Sandbox Environment — run_in_sandbox
text: Invisible NULL. function called side effects.

what: Setup a Sandbox Environment for Testing — setup_sandbox

text: files Character vector relative file directory paths copy sandbox.  Paths resolved relative project root (found using find_root()); project root determined current working directory used.  path refers directory, entire directory copied recursively.  Absolute paths path traversal attempts (e.g., ..) rejected security. Snapshot files need listed : snapshot() always reads snapshots project root, sandbox. temp_base Optional. Custom location temporary directory. NULL (default), uses tempfile().

title: Interactive Snapshot Testing — snapshot
text: Creates updates snapshot R object interactive analysis. first use, saves object human-readable snapshot file (.md). subsequent uses, compares current object saved snapshot.

title: Interactive Snapshot Testing — snapshot
code: snapshot(value, name, script_name = NULL, method = c("both", "print", "str"))

title: Interactive Snapshot Testing — snapshot
text: value R object snapshot (e.g., plot, table, model output). name Character. descriptive name snapshot. script_name Optional. name script creating snapshot. NULL, attempts auto-detect call stack. method Character. Controls serialization method(s) used capturing snapshot. "" (default) applies type-specific logic uses print() str(). "print" uses print(), "str" uses str(). Use "print" "str" one methods produces volatile output excluded snapshot (e.g. objects embed session-specific paths IDs str() representation).

title: Interactive Snapshot Testing — snapshot
text: Invisible TRUE snapshot matches updated. testing mode, throws error snapshot missing match.

title: Interactive Snapshot Testing — snapshot
text: interactive mode (default), prompts user update differences found emits warning. testing mode (inside testthat run_in_sandbox), throws error snapshot exist match. Snapshots stored _resultcheck_snapshots/ directory relative project root, organized script name.

title: Interactive Snapshot Testing — snapshot
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # In an analysis script (interactive mode): model <- lm(mpg ~ wt, data = mtcars) snapshot(model, "mtcars_model")  # First time: saves the snapshot # Later times: compares, shows differences, prompts to update  # Use only print() output (skip str() which may contain volatile fields): snapshot(model, "mtcars_model_print", method = "print")  # Use only str() output: snapshot(model, "mtcars_model_str", method = "str")  # In testing mode (inside run_in_sandbox or testthat): # Errors if snapshot missing or doesn't match } # }

title: resultcheck 0.1.3
text: Prepare release CRAN.

title: resultcheck 0.1.2
text: Add precision rounding option resultcheck.yml stabilise snapshot comparisons across runs. Add [ignored] markers snapshot() output explicitly indicate excluded components.

title: resultcheck 0.1.1
text: Fixed snapshot inconsistencies caused variable serialization width directory checks, improving reliability result comparisons. Added method parameter snapshot() print/str/serialization.

title: resultcheck 0.1.0
text: snapshot() — create verify human-readable snapshots R objects (models, data frames, tables, vectors, etc.). Interactive mode warns prompts; testing mode (inside run_in_sandbox()) errors mismatch. setup_sandbox() — copy project files /directories temporary directory isolated testing. Accepts individual file paths entire subdirectories. run_in_sandbox() — execute R script inside sandbox working directory set sandbox root, find_root() snapshot() transparently resolve back original project root. cleanup_sandbox() — remove sandbox directory testing. find_root() — locate project root using resultcheck.yml, .Rproj, .git markers.
